title: Help Yusuf recover from cardiac surgery
story:
My name is Yusuf Abdullah, and I am reaching out on behalf of my family during the most frightening period of our lives. On March 14, 2026, I was rushed by ambulance to Mercy General Hospital in Columbus, Ohio, after collapsing at work with severe chest pain. After a battery of tests including an echocardiogram and cardiac catheterization, Dr. Sarah Nguyen diagnosed me with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) — a serious condition in which the heart muscle thickens, making it harder for the heart to pump blood. The cardiologist determined I required an implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) to prevent sudden cardiac death, along with an ongoing regimen of beta-blockers and quarterly monitoring.

The procedure was performed on March 21, 2026, at Mercy General's Cardiac Care Unit. Total medical costs — including the 6-night inpatient stay, the ICD device, the catheterization lab fees, and post-operative medication — came to $47,840. My employer-provided insurance (BlueCross BlueShield of Ohio) covered $31,200 after negotiated rates, leaving a patient responsibility of $16,640. Because I have been unable to return to work since the procedure (I was employed as a warehouse supervisor at Amazon's DHL3 facility in Groveport, OH), my family has exhausted our emergency savings covering rent and groceries for our household of five, including three children ages 4, 8, and 12.

We have already applied for Mercy General's financial assistance program and received a $2,000 grant, reducing our outstanding balance to $14,640. We are asking the community to help cover the remaining amount so we can close this medical debt and focus on my recovery. Any funds raised beyond the target will go toward ongoing cardiac monitoring appointments and medication costs for the next 12 months. I have uploaded my government-issued ID, a recent bank statement, and the itemized hospital bill from Mercy General as supporting documentation.

JazakAllah khayr for your generosity and du'a.
